data.title: Distributed Systems
data.description: Instagram employs a distributed architecture to handle its large scale and ensure reliability and uptime.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes load balancers, microservices, and data replication.

data.title: Microservices
data.description: A shift from monolithic to microservices architecture allows for independent deployment and scaling.
data.technical_spec: Decomposed services handling different functionalities such as user media, notifications.

data.title: Graph Databases
data.description: Instagram leverages graph databases to efficiently manage and query complex and interconnected data.
data.technical_spec: Use of technologies like Apollo (GraphQL) for querying relational data structures.

data.title: Cloud-Based Infrastructure
data.description: Relying on Facebook's data centers, Instagram is hosted on cloud platforms ensuring flexibility and scalability.

data.title: CDN Utilization
data.description: Content delivery networks are employed to reduce latency and serve content quickly to global users.
data.technical_spec: Akamai, Cloudflare, or in-house solutions for high-speed delivery.
